text: Robust multi-object tracking (MOT) in video sequences requires sophisticated modeling of spatial and temporal interactions between targets. In this paper, we introduce TransMOT, a tracking framework that utilizes graph transformers to effectively capture these complex relationships. To manage a large number of targets efficiently, TransMOT represents object trajectories as a set of sparse weighted graphs. Building upon this structural representation, our architecture incorporates a spatial graph transformer encoder, a temporal transformer encoder, and a spatial graph transformer decoder. This design not only improves computational efficiency compared to standard Transformer models but also enhances tracking accuracy by exploiting the inherent spatial structure of the scene. To further optimize performance, we introduce a cascade association framework designed to handle low-score detections and long-term occlusions, which are typically resource-intensive to model directly. We evaluate TransMOT on several prominent benchmarks, including MOT15, MOT16, MOT17, and MOT20. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance across all datasets, demonstrating its effectiveness and robustness in diverse and crowded environments.
